step_by_step_thinking: \boxed{D} Myasthenia gravis is a disorder of the neuromuscular junction, specifically involving autoantibodies that target acetylcholine receptors (AChRs) or mu-sk at the NMJ, disrupting neuromuscular transmission and leading to muscle weakness. This is consistently supported by references stating it is an autoimmune disorder affecting the neuromuscular junction (NMJ) and that the primary pathology involves interference with signaling at the NMJ. Therefore, option D is correct.
raw_response:
\boxed{D} Myasthenia gravis is a disorder of the neuromuscular junction, specifically involving autoantibodies that target acetylcholine receptors (AChRs) or mu-sk at the NMJ, disrupting neuromuscular transmission and leading to muscle weakness. This is consistently supported by references stating it is an autoimmune disorder affecting the neuromuscular junction (NMJ) and that the primary pathology involves interference with signaling at the NMJ. Therefore, option D is correct.
\boxed{D}
